title: Calendar Slot Table
description: The calendar slot-table contract (RD-5 / DD-22). The calendar ships as markdown with a schema'd slot-row contract; this schema defines one calendar's metadata plus its array of recurring weekly slots. Calendar owns the clock time assigned to each slot. Grounded in the production slot-table columns. Spec ref: release-spec.md §6.5.

schema_version.description: Calendar shape version, for migration handling (§11.1).

timezone.description: IANA timezone the slot 'time' values are expressed in (timezone-explicit; the production time_et column generalized). Defaults to UTC when absent.

slots.description: Recurring weekly slots. Each row is one scheduled cadence entry.

slot_id.description: Unique, stable, human-readable id used for weekly dedup. Convention: <brand>-<day>-<pos>.

brand.description: Brand id this slot belongs to (the production 'account' column, generalized to the brand-keyed model).

day.description: Day of week the slot recurs.

time.description: Clock time the slot fires, 24-hour HH:MM, in the calendar 'timezone' (DD-22: the calendar owns clock time).

pillar.description: Content pillar / theme lane label (operator-defined taxonomy). Optional.

theme.description: Optional explicit theme for this slot, overriding the pillar default.

content_type.description: Content-type label for the slot (operator/platform vocabulary, e.g. tweet-text, thread, feed-single, reel, short-clip, reaction-gif).

command_family.description: Operator-command family this slot dispatches (see operator-command schema; aligned with campaign.schema.json command_families).

format.description: The FORMAT value passed to the operator command (e.g. 'single tweet', 'thread', 'feed single', 'reel', 'short clip', 'reaction gif').

slot_type.description: Slot category. trend slots are RESERVED for the config-gated trend pass (DD-16: trend content fills these, never out-of-calendar); work_recap slots are RESERVED for the config-gated daily build-in-public option (§3.3, operator/founder/team accounts); campaign slots are claimable by a campaign's slot_pattern; regular is the default pillar-driven slot. Both trend and work_recap pathways ship OFF by default; reserving a slot only enables filling once the matching config block is enabled.

state.description: active means the scheduler fires this slot; dormant means it is skipped (reason belongs in notes).

notes.description: Free text: theme hint, constraint, or reason for dormancy.
